text.DRB: And these are the Princes according to their kindreds, and several principalities of them that came up from Babylon the Kingdom of Artaxerxes.
text.KJV: Of the sons of Pahath Moab, Eliaonias, the son of Zaraias, and with him two hundred men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Pahath-moab, Eliehoenai son of Zerahiah, and with him two hundred men.

text.DRB: Of the children of Phares, Gersomus: and of the children of Siemarith, Amenus: of the children of David, Acchus the son of Scecilia:
text.KJV: Of the sons of Zathoe, Sechenias the son of Jezelus, and with him three hundred men: of the sons of Adin, Obeth the son of Jonathan, and with him two hundred and fifty men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Zattu, Shecaniah son of Jahaziel, and with him three hundred men. Of the descendants of Adin, Obed son of Jonathan, and with him two hundred and fifty men.

text.DRB: Of the children of Phares, Zacharias, and with him returned an hundred fifty men.
text.KJV: Of the sons of Elam, Josias son of Gotholias, and with him seventy men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Elam, Jeshaiah son of Gotholiah, and with him seventy men.

text.DRB: Of the children of leader Moabilion, Zaraei, and with him two hundred fifty men.
text.KJV: Of the sons of Saphatias, Zaraias son of Michael, and with him threescore and ten men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Shephatiah, Zeraiah son of Michael, and with him seventy men.

text.DRB: Of the children of Zachues, Iechonias of Zechoel, and with him two hundred fifty men:
text.KJV: Of the sons of Joab, Abadias son of Jezelus, and with him two hundred and twelve men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Joab, Obadiah son of Jehiel, and with him two hundred and twelve men.

text.DRB: of the children of Sala, Maasias of Gotholia, & with him seventy men:
text.KJV: Of the sons of Banid, Assalimoth son of Josaphias, and with him an hundred and threescore men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Bani, Shelomith son of Josiphiah, and with him a hundred and sixty men.

text.DRB: of the children of Saphatia, Zarias of Michel, and with him eighty men:
text.KJV: Of the sons of Babi, Zacharias son of Bebai, and with him twenty and eight men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Bebai, Zechariah son of Bebai, and with him twenty-eight men.

text.DRB: of the children of Iob, Abdias of Iehel, and with him two hundred twelve men:
text.KJV: Of the sons of Astath, Johannes son of Acatan, and with him an hundred and ten men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Azgad, Johanan son of Hakkatan, and with him a hundred and ten men.

text.DRB: of the children of Bania, Salimoth, the son of Iosaphia, and with him an hundred sixty men:
text.KJV: Of the sons of Adonikam the last, and these are the names of them, Eliphalet, Jewel, and Samaias, and with them seventy men:
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Adonikam, the last ones, their names being Eliphelet, Jeuel, and Shemaiah, and with them seventy men.

text.DRB: of the children of Beer, Zacharias Bebei, and with him two hundred eight men:
text.KJV: Of the sons of Bago, Uthi the son of Istalcurus, and with him seventy men.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Bigvai, Uthai son of Istalcurus, and with him seventy men.

text.DRB: of the children of Ezead, Ioannes of Eccetan, and with him an hundred ten men:
text.KJV: And these I gathered together to the river called Theras, where we pitched our tents three days: and then I surveyed them.
text.NRSV: I assembled them at the river called Theras, and we encamped there for three days, and I inspected them.

text.DRB: of the children of Adonicam, which were last, and these are their names, Eliphalam the son of Gebel, and Semeias, and with him seventy men.
text.KJV: But when I had found there none of the priests and Levites,
text.NRSV: When I found there none of the descendants of the priests or of the Levites,

text.DRB: And I gathered them together to the river that is called Thia, and we camped there three days, and viewed them again.
text.KJV: Then sent I unto Eleazar, and Iduel, and Masman,
text.NRSV: I sent word to Eliezar, Iduel, Maasmas,

text.DRB: And of the children of the Priests and Levites I found not there.
text.KJV: And Alnathan, and Mamaias, and Joribas, and Nathan, Eunatan, Zacharias, and Mosollamon, principal men and learned.
text.NRSV: Elnathan, Shemaiah, Jarib, Nathan, Elnathan, Zechariah, and Meshullam, who were leaders and men of understanding;

text.DRB: And I sent to Eleazarus, and Eccelon, and Masman, and Maloban, and Enaathan, and Samea, and Ioribum, Nathan, Enuagam, Zacharias, and Mosolam the leaders themselves, and that were skillful.
text.KJV: And I bade them that they should go unto Saddeus the captain, who was in the place of the treasury:
text.NRSV: I told them to go to Iddo, who was the leading man at the place of the treasury,

text.DRB: And I said to them that they should come to Loddeus, who was at the place of the treasury.
text.KJV: And commanded them that they should speak unto Daddeus, and to his brethren, and to the treasurers in that place, to send us such men as might execute the priests’ office in the house of the Lord.
text.NRSV: and ordered them to tell Iddo and his kindred and the treasurers at that place to send us men to serve as priests in the house of our Lord.

text.DRB: And I commanded them to say to Loddeus, and his brethren, and to them that were in the treasury, that they should send us them that might do the function of priesthood in the house of the Lord our God.
text.KJV: And by the mighty hand of our Lord they brought unto us skilful men of the sons of Moli the son of Levi, the son of Israel, Asebebia, and his sons, and his brethren, who were eighteen.
text.NRSV: And by the mighty hand of our Lord they brought us competent men of the descendants of Mahli son of Levi, son of Israel, namely Sherebiah] with his descendants and kinsmen, eighteen;

text.DRB: And they brought unto us according to the mighty hand of the Lord our God cunning men: of the children of Moholi, the son of Levi, the son of Israel, Sebebia, & his sons and brethren, which were eighteen:
text.KJV: And Asebia, and Annus, and Osaias his brother, of the sons of Channuneus, and their sons, were twenty men.
text.NRSV: also Hashabiah and Annunus and his brother Jeshaiah, of the descendants of Hananiah, and their descendants, twenty men;

text.DRB: Asbia, and Amin of the sons of the children of Chananeus, and their children twenty men.
text.KJV: And of the servants of the temple whom David had ordained, and the principal men for the service of the Levites to wit, the servants of the temple two hundred and twenty, the catalogue of whose names were shewed.
text.NRSV: and of the temple servants, whom David and the leaders had given for the service of the Levites, two hundred and twenty temple servants; the list of all their names was reported.

text.DRB: And of them that served the temple, whom David gave, and the princes themselves to the ministry of the Levites of them that served the temple, two hundred twenty. All their names were signified in writings.
text.KJV: And there I vowed a fast unto the young men before our Lord, to desire of him a prosperous journey both for us and them that were with us, for our children, and for the cattle:
text.NRSV: There I proclaimed a fast for the young men before our Lord, to seek from him a prosperous journey for ourselves and for our children and the livestock that were with us.

text.DRB: And I vowed there a fast to the young men in the sight of God, that I might ask of him a good journey for us, and them that were with us, and for the children, and the cattle because of ambushments.
text.KJV: For I was ashamed to ask the king footmen, and horsemen, and conduct for safeguard against our adversaries.
text.NRSV: For I was ashamed to ask the king for foot-soldiers and cavalry and an escort to keep us safe from our adversaries;

text.DRB: For I was ashamed to ask of the king footmen and horsemen in my company, to guard us, against our adversaries.
text.KJV: For we had said unto the king, that the power of the Lord our God should be with them that seek him, to support them in all ways.
text.NRSV: for we had said to the king, ‘The power of our Lord will be with those who seek him, and will support them in every way.’

text.DRB: For we said to the king that the power of our Lord will be with them that seek him with all affection.
text.KJV: And again we besought our Lord as touching these things, and found him favourable unto us.
text.NRSV: And again we prayed to our Lord about these things, and we found him very merciful.

text.DRB: And again we besought the Lord our God according to these things: whom also we had propitious, and we obtained of our God.
text.KJV: Then I separated twelve of the chief of the priests, Esebrias, and Assanias, and ten men of their brethren with them:
text.NRSV: Then I set apart twelve of the leaders of the priests, Sherebiah and Hashabiah, and ten of their kinsmen with them;

text.DRB: And I separated of the rulers of the people, and of the Priests of the temple, twelve men, and Sedebia, and Asanna, and with them of their brethren ten men.
text.KJV: And I weighed them the gold, and the silver, and the holy vessels of the house of our Lord, which the king, and his council, and the princes, and all Israel, had given.
text.NRSV: and I weighed out to them the silver and the gold and the holy vessels of the house of our Lord, which the king himself and his counsellors and the nobles and all Israel had given.

text.DRB: And I weighed to them the gold and silver, and the vessels of the house of our God pertaining to the Priests, which the king had given, and his counsellers, and the princes, and all Israel.
text.KJV: And when I had weighed it, I delivered unto them six hundred and fifty talents of silver, and silver vessels of an hundred talents, and an hundred talents of gold,
text.NRSV: I weighed and gave to them six hundred and fifty talents of silver, and silver vessels worth a hundred talents, and a hundred talents of gold,

text.DRB: And when I had weighed it, I delivered of silver an hundred fifty talents, and silver vessels of an hundred talents, and of gold an hundred talents.
text.KJV: And twenty golden vessels, and twelve vessels of brass, even of fine brass, glittering like gold.
text.NRSV: and twenty golden bowls, and twelve bronze vessels of fine bronze that glittered like gold.

text.DRB: And of vessels of gold seven score and twelve brazen vessels good of shining brass, resembling the form of gold.
text.KJV: And I said unto them, Both ye are holy unto the Lord, and the vessels are holy, and the gold and the silver is a vow unto the Lord, the Lord of our fathers.
text.NRSV: And I said to them, ‘You are holy to the Lord, and the vessels are holy, and the silver and the gold are vowed to the Lord, the Lord of our ancestors.

text.DRB: And I said to them: You are also sanctified to our Lord, and the vessels be holy, and the gold and silver is vowed to our Lord the God of our fathers.
text.KJV: Watch ye, and keep them till ye deliver them to the chief of the priests and Levites, and to the principal men of the families of Israel, in Jerusalem, into the chambers of the house of our God.
text.NRSV: Be watchful and on guard until you deliver them to the leaders of the priests and the Levites, and to the heads of the ancestral houses of Israel, in Jerusalem, in the chambers of the house of our Lord.’

text.DRB: And those Priests and Levites that received the gold and silver and vessels, brought it to Jerusalem into the temple of our Lord.
text.KJV: And from the river Theras we departed the twelfth day of the first month, and came to Jerusalem by the mighty hand of our Lord, which was with us: and from the beginning of our journey the Lord delivered us from every enemy, and so we came to Jerusalem.
text.NRSV: We left the river Theras on the twelfth day of the first month; and we arrived in Jerusalem by the mighty hand of our Lord, which was upon us; he delivered us from every enemy on the way, and so we came to Jerusalem.

text.DRB: And we went forward from the river Thia, the twelfth day of the first month, till we entered into Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And when we had been there three days, the gold and silver that was weighed was delivered in the house of our Lord on the fourth day unto Marmoth the priest the son of Iri.
text.NRSV: When we had been there for three days, the silver and the gold were weighed and delivered in the house of our Lord to the priest Meremoth son of Uriah;

text.DRB: And when the third day was come, in the fourth day the gold being weighed, and the silver, was delivered in the house of the Lord our God, to Marimoth Priest the son of Iori.
text.KJV: And with him was Eleazar the son of Phinees, and with them were Josabad the son of Jesu and Moeth the son of Sabban, Levites: all was delivered them by number and weight.
text.NRSV: with him was Eleazar son of Phinehas, and with them were Jozabad son of Jeshua and Moeth son of Binnui,] the Levites.

text.DRB: And with him was Eleazar the son of Phinees: and with them were Iosadus the son of Iesus, and Medias, and Banni the son of a Levite, by number and weight all things.
text.KJV: And all the weight of them was written up the same hour.
text.NRSV: The whole was counted and weighed, and the weight of everything was recorded at that very time.

text.DRB: And the weight of them was written the same hour.
text.KJV: Moreover they that were come out of the captivity offered sacrifice unto the Lord God of Israel, even twelve bullocks for all Israel, fourscore and sixteen rams,
text.NRSV: And those who had returned from exile offered sacrifices to the Lord, the God of Israel, twelve bulls for all Israel, ninety-six rams,

text.DRB: And they that came out of the captivity, offered sacrifice to our Lord the God of Israel, oxen twelve, for all Israel, rams eighty six,
text.KJV: Threescore and twelve lambs, goats for a peace offering, twelve; all of them a sacrifice to the Lord.
text.NRSV: seventy-two lambs, and as a thank-offering twelve male goats—all as a sacrifice to the Lord.

text.DRB: lambs seventy two, buck goats for sin twelve, and for health twelve kine, all for the sacrifice of our Lord.
text.KJV: And they delivered the king’s commandments unto the king’s stewards’ and to the governors of Celosyria and Phenice; and they honoured the people and the temple of God.
text.NRSV: They delivered the king’s orders to the royal stewards and to the governors of Coelesyria and Phoenicia; and these officials] honoured the people and the temple of the Lord.

text.DRB: And they read again the precepts of the king to the kings' officers, and to the deputies of Coelesyria, and Phoenice: and they honored the nation, and the temple of our Lord.
text.KJV: Now when these things were done, the rulers came unto me, and said,
text.NRSV: After these things had been done, the leaders came to me and said,

text.DRB: And these things being finished, the rulers came to me, saying: The stock of Israel, and the princes, and the Priests, and the Levites
text.KJV: The nation of Israel, the princes, the priests and Levites, have not put away from them the strange people of the land, nor the pollutions of the Gentiles to wit, of the Canaanites, Hittites, Pheresites, Jebusites, and the Moabites, Egyptians, and Edomites.
text.NRSV: ‘The people of Israel and the rulers and the priests and the Levites have not put away from themselves the alien peoples of the land and their pollutions, the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Jebusites, the Moabites, the Egyptians, and the Edomites.

text.DRB: and the strange people, and nations of the land have not separated their uncleanness from the Chananeites, and Hetheites, and Pherezeites, and Iebuseites, and Moobites, & Ægyptians, and Idumeians.
text.KJV: For both they and their sons have married with their daughters, and the holy seed is mixed with the strange people of the land; and from the beginning of this matter the rulers and the great men have been partakers of this iniquity.
text.NRSV: For they and their descendants have married the daughters of these people,] and the holy race has been mixed with the alien peoples of the land; and from the beginning of this matter the leaders and the nobles have been sharing in this iniquity.’

text.DRB: For they are joined to their daughters both themselves, and their sons: and the holy seed is mingled with the strange nations of the earth, and the rulers and magistrates were partakers of that iniquity from the beginning of the reign itself.
text.KJV: And as soon as I had heard these things, I rent my clothes, and the holy garment, and pulled off the hair from off my head and beard, and sat me down sad and very heavy.
text.NRSV: As soon as I heard these things I tore my garments and my holy mantle, and pulled out hair from my head and beard, and sat down in anxiety and grief.

text.DRB: And forthwith as I heard these things, I rent my garments and the sacred tunic: and tearing the hairs of my head, and my beard, I sat sorrowful and heavy.
text.KJV: So all they that were then moved at the word of the Lord God of Israel assembled unto me, whilst I mourned for the iniquity: but I sat still full of heaviness until the evening sacrifice.
text.NRSV: And all who were ever moved at] the word of the Lord of Israel gathered around me, as I mourned over this iniquity, and I sat grief-stricken until the evening sacrifice.

text.DRB: And there assembled to me mourning upon this iniquity, as many as were then moved by the word of our Lord the God of Israel, and I sat sad until the evening sacrifice.
text.KJV: Then rising up from the fast with my clothes and the holy garment rent, and bowing my knees, and stretching forth my hands unto the Lord,
text.NRSV: Then I rose from my fast, with my garments and my holy mantle torn, and kneeling down and stretching out my hands to the Lord

text.DRB: And I rising up from fasting, having my garments rent and the sacred tunic, kneeling, and stretching forth my hands to our Lord,
text.KJV: I said, O Lord, I am confounded and ashamed before thy face;
text.NRSV: ‘O Lord, I am ashamed and confused before your face.

text.DRB: I said: Lord I am confounded, and ashamed before thy face,
text.KJV: For our sins are multiplied above our heads, and our ignorances have reached up unto heaven.
text.NRSV: For our sins have risen higher than our heads, and our mistakes have mounted up to heaven

text.DRB: for our sins are multiplied over our heads, and our iniquities are exalted even to heaven.
text.KJV: For ever since the time of our fathers we have been and are in great sin, even unto this day.
text.NRSV: from the times of our ancestors, and we are in great sin to this day.

text.DRB: Because from the times of our fathers we are in great sin unto this day.
text.KJV: And for our sins and our fathers’ we with our brethren and our kings and our priests were given up unto the kings of the earth, to the sword, and to captivity, and for a prey with shame, unto this day.
text.NRSV: Because of our sins and the sins of our ancestors, we with our kindred and our kings and our priests were given over to the kings of the earth, to the sword and exile and plundering, in shame until this day.

text.DRB: And for the sins of us, and of our fathers we have been delivered with our brethren, and with our Priests to the kings of the earth, into sword and captivity, and spoil with confusion unto this present day.
text.KJV: And now in some measure hath mercy been shewed unto us from thee, O Lord, that there should be left us a root and a name in the place of thy sanctuary;
text.NRSV: And now in some measure mercy has come to us from you, O Lord, to leave to us a root and a name in your holy place,

text.DRB: And now what a great thing is this that mercy hath happened to us from thee, o Lord God, & leave thou unto us a root, and a name in the place of thy sanctification,
text.KJV: And to discover unto us a light in the house of the Lord our God, and to give us food in the time of our servitude.
text.NRSV: and to uncover a light for us in the house of the Lord our God, and to give us food in the time of our servitude.

text.DRB: to discover our light in the house of the Lord our God, to give us meat in all the time of our bondage.
text.KJV: Yea, when we were in bondage, we were not forsaken of our Lord; but he made us gracious before the kings of Persia, so that they gave us food;
text.NRSV: Even in our bondage we were not forsaken by our Lord, but he brought us into favour with the kings of the Persians, so that they have given us food

text.DRB: And when we served, we were not forsaken of the Lord our God: but he set us in favour, appointing the kings of the Persians to give us meat,
text.KJV: Yea, and honoured the temple of our Lord, and raised up the desolate Sion, that they have given us a sure abiding in Jewry and Jerusalem.
text.NRSV: and glorified the temple of our Lord, and raised Zion from desolation, to give us a stronghold in Judea and Jerusalem.

text.DRB: and to glorify the temple of the Lord our God, and to build the desolations of Sion, to give us stability in Jury, and Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And now, O Lord, what shall we say, having these things? for we have transgressed thy commandments, which thou gavest by the hand of thy servants the prophets, saying,
text.NRSV: ‘And now, O Lord, what shall we say, when we have these things? For we have transgressed your commandments, which you gave by your servants the prophets, saying,

text.DRB: And now what say we Lord, having these things? For we have transgressed thy precepts, which thou gavest into the hands of thy servants the prophets,
text.KJV: That the land, which ye enter into to possess as an heritage, is a land polluted with the pollutions of the strangers of the land, and they have filled it with their uncleanness.
text.NRSV: “The land that you are entering to take possession of is a land polluted with the pollution of the aliens of the land, and they have filled it with their uncleanness.

text.DRB: saying: That the land into which ye entered to possess the inheritance thereof, is a land polluted with the coinquinations of the strangers of the land, and their uncleanness hath filled it wholly with their filthiness.
text.KJV: Therefore now shall ye not join your daughters unto their sons, neither shall ye take their daughters unto your sons.
text.NRSV: Therefore do not give your daughters in marriage to their descendants, and do not take their daughters for your descendants;

text.DRB: And now your daughters you shall not match with their sons, and their daughters you shall not take for your sons.
text.KJV: Moreover ye shall never seek to have peace with them, that ye may be strong, and eat the good things of the land, and that ye may leave the inheritance of the land unto your children for evermore.
text.NRSV: do not seek ever to have peace with them, so that you may be strong and eat the good things of the land and leave it for an inheritance to your children for ever.”

text.DRB: And you shall not seek to have peace with them for ever, that growing strong you may eat the best things of the land, and may distribute the inheritance to your children for ever.
text.KJV: And all that is befallen is done unto us for our wicked works and great sins; for thou, O Lord, didst make our sins light,
text.NRSV: And all that has happened to us has come about because of our evil deeds and our great sins. For you, O Lord, lifted the burden of our sins

text.DRB: And the things that happen to us, all are done for our naughty works, and our great sins.
text.KJV: And didst give unto us such a root: but we have turned back again to transgress thy law, and to mingle ourselves with the uncleanness of the nations of the land.
text.NRSV: and gave us such a root as this; but we turned back again to transgress your law by mixing with the uncleanness of the peoples of the land.

text.DRB: And thou gavest us such a root, and we are returned again to transgress thy ordinances, that we would be mingled with the uncleanness of the nations of this land.
text.KJV: Mightest not thou be angry with us to destroy us, till thou hadst left us neither root, seed, nor name?
text.NRSV: Were you not angry enough with us to destroy us without leaving a root or seed or name?

text.DRB: Wilt not thou be wrath with us to destroy us, till there be no root left nor our name?
text.KJV: O Lord of Israel, thou art true: for we are left a root this day.
text.NRSV: O Lord of Israel, you are faithful; for we are left as a root to this day.

text.DRB: Lord God of Israel thou art true. For there is a root left until this present day.
text.KJV: Behold, now are we before thee in our iniquities, for we cannot stand any longer by reason of these things before thee.
text.NRSV: See, we are now before you in our iniquities; for we can no longer stand in your presence because of these things.’

text.KJV: And as Esdras in his prayer made his confession, weeping, and lying flat upon the ground before the temple, there gathered unto him from Jerusalem a very great multitude of men and women and children: for there was great weeping among the multitude.
text.NRSV: While Ezra was praying and making his confession, weeping and lying face down on the ground before the temple, a very great multitude of men, women, and children gathered around him from Jerusalem; for there was great weeping among the people.
text.DRB: Behold, now we are in

And when Esdras with adoration confessed weeping, lying flat on the ground before the temple, there were gathered before him out of Jerusalem a very great multitude, men and women, and young men and young women. For there was great weeping in the multitude itself.
text.KJV: Then Jechonias the son of Jeelus, one of the sons of Israel, called out, and said, O Esdras, we have sinned against the Lord God, we have married strange women of the nations of the land, and now is all Israel aloft.
text.NRSV: Then Shecaniah son of Jehiel, one of the men of Israel, called out, and said to Ezra, ‘We have sinned against the Lord, and have married foreign women from the peoples of the land; but even now there is hope for Israel.

text.DRB: And when he had cried, Iechonias of Ieheli of the children of Israel, said to Esdras: We have sinned against our Lord, for that we have taken unto us in marriage strange women of the nations of the land.
text.KJV: Let us make an oath to the Lord, that we will put away all our wives, which we have taken of the heathen, with their children,
text.NRSV: Let us take an oath to the Lord about this, that we will put away all our foreign wives, with their children,

text.DRB: And now thou art over all Israel, in these therefore let there be an oath from our Lord to expel all our wives that are of strangers with their children.
text.KJV: Like as thou hast decreed, and as many as do obey the law of the Lord.
text.NRSV: as seems good to you and to all who obey the law of the Lord.

text.DRB: As it was decreed to thee of the ancesters according to the law of our Lord, rising up declare it.
text.KJV: Arise and put in execution: for to thee doth this matter appertain, and we will be with thee: do valiantly.
text.NRSV: Rise up] and take action, for it is your task, and we are with you to take strong measures.’

text.DRB: For to thee the business pertaineth, and we are with thee: do manfully.
text.KJV: So Esdras arose, and took an oath of the chief of the priests and Levites of all Israel to do after these things; and so they sware.
text.NRSV: Then Ezra rose up and made the leaders of the priests and Levites of all Israel swear that they would do this. And they swore to it.

text.DRB: And Esdras rising up adjured the princes of the Priests and Levites, and all Israel to do according to these things and they sware.

text.KJV: Then Esdras rising from the court of the temple went to the chamber of Joanan the son of Eliasib,
text.NRSV: Then Ezra rose and went from the court of the temple to the chamber of Jehohanan son of Eliashib.
text.DRB: And Esdras rising up from before the court of the temple, went into the chamber of Ionathas the son of Nasabi.
And lodging there he tasted no bread, nor drank water for the iniquity of the multitude.
text.KJV: And remained there, and did eat no meat nor drink water, mourning for the great iniquities of the multitude.
text.NRSV: and spent the night there; and he did not eat bread or drink water, for he was mourning over the great iniquities of the multitude.

text.DRB: And there was proclamation made in all Jury, & in Jerusalem to all that were of the captivity gathered in Jerusalem,
text.KJV: And there was a proclamation in all Jewry and Jerusalem to all them that were of the captivity, that they should be gathered together at Jerusalem:
text.NRSV: And a proclamation was made throughout Judea and Jerusalem to all who had returned from exile that they should assemble at Jerusalem,

text.DRB: that whosoever shall not appear with in two or three days, according to the judgement of the ancients sitting upon it, their goods should be taken away, and himself should be judged an alien from the multitude of the captivity.
text.KJV: And that whosoever met not there within two or three days according as the elders that bare rule appointed, their cattle should be seized to the use of the temple, and himself cast out from them that were of the captivity.
text.NRSV: and that if any did not meet there within two or three days, in accordance with the decision of the ruling elders, their livestock would be seized for sacrifice and the men themselves] expelled from the multitude of those who had returned from the captivity.

text.DRB: And all were gathered that were of the tribe of Juda, and of Benjamin within three days in Jerusalem: this is the ninth month, the twentieth day of the month.
text.KJV: And in three days were all they of the tribe of Judah and Benjamin gathered together at Jerusalem the twentieth day of the ninth month.
text.NRSV: Then the men of the tribe of Judah and Benjamin assembled at Jerusalem within three days; this was the ninth month, on the twentieth day of the month.

text.DRB: And all the multitude sat in the court of the temple trembling, for the present winter.
text.KJV: And all the multitude sat trembling in the broad court of the temple because of the present foul weather.
text.NRSV: All the multitude sat in the open square before the temple, shivering because of the bad weather that prevailed.

text.DRB: And Esdras rising up said to them: You have done unlawfully taking to you in marriage strange wives, that you might add to the sins of Israel.
text.KJV: So Esdras arose up, and said unto them, Ye have transgressed the law in marrying strange wives, thereby to increase the sins of Israel.
text.NRSV: Then Ezra stood up and said to them, ‘You have broken the law and married foreign women, and so have increased the sin of Israel.

text.DRB: And now give confession, & magnificence to our Lord the God of our fathers:
text.KJV: And now by confessing give glory unto the Lord God of our fathers,
text.NRSV: Now then, make confession and give glory to the Lord the God of our ancestors,

text.DRB: and accomplish his will, and depart from the nations of the land, and from your wives the strangers.
text.KJV: And do his will, and separate yourselves from the heathen of the land, and from the strange women.
text.NRSV: and do his will; separate yourselves from the peoples of the land and from your foreign wives.’

text.DRB: And all the multitude cried, and they said with a loud voice: As thou hast said, we will do.
text.KJV: Then cried the whole multitude, and said with a loud voice, Like as thou hast spoken, so will we do.
text.NRSV: Then all the multitude shouted and said with a loud voice, ‘We will do as you have said.

text.DRB: But because the multitude is great, and winter time, and we cannot stand in the air without succour: and this is a work for us not of one day, nor of two, for we have sinned much in these things:
text.KJV: But forasmuch as the people are many, and it is foul weather, so that we cannot stand without, and this is not a work of a day or two, seeing our sin in these things is spread far:
text.NRSV: But the multitude is great and it is winter, and we are not able to stand in the open air. This is not a work we can do in one day or two, for we have sinned too much in these things.

text.DRB: Let the rulers of the multitude stand, and that dwell with us, and as many as have with them foreign wives,
text.KJV: Therefore let the rulers of the multitude stay, and let all them of our habitations that have strange wives come at the time appointed,
text.NRSV: So let the leaders of the multitude stay, and let all those in our settlements who have foreign wives come at the time appointed,

text.DRB: and at a time appointed let the priests out of every place, and the judges assist, until they appease the wrath of our Lord concerning this business.
text.KJV: And with them the rulers and judges of every place, till we turn away the wrath of the Lord from us for this matter.
text.NRSV: with the elders and judges of each place, until we are freed from the wrath of the Lord over this matter.’

text.DRB: And Ionathas the son of Ezeli, and Ozias of Thecam took upon them according to these words: and Bosoramus, and Levis, and Sabbathæus, wrought together with them.
text.KJV: Then Jonathan the son of Azael and Ezechias the son of Theocanus accordingly took this matter upon them: and Mosollam and Levis and Sabbatheus helped them.
text.NRSV: Jonathan son of Asahel and Jahzeiah son of Tikvah] undertook the matter on these terms, and Meshullam and Levi and Shabbethai served with them as judges.

text.DRB: And all that were of the captivity stood according to all these things.
text.KJV: And they that were of the captivity did according to all these things.
text.NRSV: And those who had returned from exile acted in accordance with all this.

text.DRB: And Esdras the priest chose unto him men the great princes of their fathers according to their names: & they sat together in the newmoon of the tenth month to examine this business.
text.KJV: And Esdras the priest chose unto him the principal men of their families, all by name: and in the first day of the tenth month they sat together to examine the matter.
text.NRSV: Ezra the priest chose for himself the leading men of their ancestral houses, all of them by name; and at the new moon of the tenth month they began their sessions to investigate the matter.

text.DRB: And they determined of the men that had outlandish wives, until the newmoon of the first month.
text.KJV: So their cause that held strange wives was brought to an end in the first day of the first month.
text.NRSV: And the cases of the men who had foreign wives were brought to an end by the new moon of the first month.

text.DRB: And there were found of the priests intermingled that had outlandish wives.
text.KJV: And of the priests that were come together, and had strange wives, there were found:
text.NRSV: Of the priests, those who were brought in and found to have foreign wives were:

text.DRB: Of the sons of Iesus the son of Iosedec, and his brethren: Maseas, and Eleazarus, and Ioribus, and Ioadeus,
text.KJV: Of the sons of Jesus the son of Josedec, and his brethren; Matthelas and Eleazar, and Joribus and Joadanus.
text.NRSV: of the descendants of Jeshua son of Jozadak and his kindred, Maaseiah, Eliezar, Jarib, and Jodan.

text.DRB: and they put to their hands to expel their wives: and to offer a ram to obtain pardon for their ignorance.
text.KJV: And they gave their hands to put away their wives and to offer rams to make reconcilement for their errors.
text.NRSV: They pledged themselves to put away their wives, and to offer rams in expiation of their error.

text.DRB: And the sons of Semmeri: Maseas and Esses, Ieelech, and Azarias.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Emmer; Ananias, and Zabdeus, and Eanes, and Sameius, and Hiereel, and Azarias.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Immer: Hanani and Zebadiah and Maaseiah and Shemaiah and Jehiel and Azariah.

text.DRB: And of the children of
text.KJV: And of the sons of Phaisur; Elionas, Massias Israel, and Nathanael, and Ocidelus and Talsas.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Pashhur: Elioenai, Maaseiah, Ishmael, and Nathanael, and Gedaliah, and Salthas.

text.DRB: And of the Levites: Iorabdus, and Semeis, and Colnis, and Calitas, and Facteas, and Coluas, and Eliomas,
text.KJV: And of the Levites; Jozabad, and Semis, and Colius, who was called Calitas, and Patheus, and Judas, and Jonas.
text.NRSV: And of the Levites: Jozabad and Shimei and Kelaiah, who was Kelita, and Pethahiah and Judah and Jonah.

text.DRB: and of the sacred singing men, Eliasib, Zaccarus.
text.KJV: Of the holy singers; Eleazurus, Bacchurus.
text.NRSV: Of the temple singers: Eliashib and Zaccur.]

text.DRB: And of the porters, Salumus, and Tolbanes.
text.KJV: Of the porters; Sallumus, and Tolbanes.
text.NRSV: Of the gatekeepers: Shallum and Telem.]

text.DRB: And of Israel: of the sons of Foro, Ozi, and Remias, and Geddias, & Melchias, and Michelus, Eleazarus, and Iammebias, and Bannas.
text.KJV: Of them of Israel, of the sons of Phoros; Hiermas, and Eddias, and Melchias, and Maelus, and Eleazar, and Asibias, and Baanias.
text.NRSV: Of Israel: of the descendants of Parosh: Ramiah, Izziah, Malchijah, Mijamin, and Eleazar, and Asibias, and Benaiah.

text.DRB: And of the sons of Iolaman: Chamas, and Zacharias, and Iezuelus, and Ioddius, and Erimoth, and Helias.
text.KJV: Of the sons of Ela; Matthanias, Zacharias, and Hierielus, and Hieremoth, and Aedias.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Elam: Mattaniah and Zechariah, Jezrielus and Abdi, and Jeremoth and Elijah.

text.DRB: And of the sons of Zathoim: Eliadas, and Liasumus, Zochias, and Larimoth, & Zabdis, and Thebedias.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Zamoth; Eliadas, Elisimus, Othonias, Jarimoth, and Sabatus, and Sardeus.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Zamoth: Eliadas, Eliashib, Othoniah, Jeremoth, and Zabad and Zerdaiah.

text.DRB: And of the sons of Zebes: Ioannes, and Amanias, and Zabdias, and Emeus.
text.KJV: Of the sons of Babai; Johannes, and Ananias and Josabad, and Amatheis.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Bebai: Jehohanan and Hananiah and Zabbai and Emathis.

text.DRB: And of the sons of Banni: Olamus, & Maluchus, and Ieddeus, and Iasub, and Azabus, & Ierimoth.
text.KJV: Of the sons of Mani; Olamus, Mamuchus, Jedeus, Jasubus, Jasael, and Hieremoth.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Mani: Olamus, Mamuchus, Adaiah, Jashub, and Sheal and Jeremoth.

text.DRB: And of the sons of Addin: Nathus, and Moosias, & Caleus, and Raanas, Maaseas, Mathathias, and Beseel, and Bonnus, and Manasses.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Addi; Naathus, and Moosias, Lacunus, and Naidus, and Mathanias, and Sesthel, Balnuus, and Manasseas.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Addi: Naathus and Moossias, Laccunus and Naidus, and Bescaspasmys and Sesthel, and Belnuus and Manasseas.

text.DRB: And of the sons of Nuæ: Noneas, and Aseas, and Melchias, and Sameas, and Simon, Benjamin, and Malchus, and Marras.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Annas; Elionas and Aseas, and Melchias, and Sabbeus, and Simon Chosameus.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Annan, Elionas and Asaias and Melchias and Sabbaias and Simon Chosamaeus.

text.DRB: And of the sons of Asom: Carianeus, Mathathias, & Bannus, & Eliphalach, and Manasses, and Semei.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Asom; Altaneus, and Matthias, and Baanaia, Eliphalet, and Manasses, and Semei.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Hashum: Mattenai and Mattattah and Zabad and Eliphelet and Manasseh and Shimei.

text.DRB: And of the sons of Banni: Ieremias, and Moadias, and Abramus, & Iohel, and Baneas, & Pelias, and Ionas, and Marimoth, & Eliasib, and Matheneus, and Eliasis, and Orizas, and Dielus, and Semedius, & Zambris, and Iosephus.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Maani; Jeremias, Momdis, Omaerus, Juel, Mabdai, and Pelias, and Anos, Carabasion, and Enasibus, and Mamnitanaimus, Eliasis, Bannus, Eliali, Samis, Selemias, Nathanias: and of the sons of Ozora; Sesis, Esril, Azaelus, Samatus, Zambis, Josephus.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Bani: Jeremai, Momdius, Maerus, Joel, Mamdai and Bedeiah and Vaniah, Carabasion and Eliashib and Mamitanemus, Eliasis, Binnui, Elialis, Shimei, Shelemiah, Nethaniah. Of the descendants of Ezora: Shashai, Azarel, Azael, Samatus, Zambris, Joseph.

text.DRB: And of the sons of Nobei: Idelus, and Mathathias, and Sabadus, and Zecheda, Zedmi, and Iessei, Baneas.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Ethma; Mazitias, Zabadaias, Edes, Juel, Banaias.
text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Nooma: Mazitias, Zabad, Iddo, Joel, Benaiah.

text.DRB: All these married outlandish wives, and did put them away with their children.
text.KJV: All these had taken strange wives, and they put them away with their children.
text.NRSV: All these had married foreign women, and they put them away together with their children.

text.KJV: And the priests and Levites, and they that were of Israel, dwelt in Jerusalem, and in the country, in the first day of the seventh month: so the children of Israel were in their habitations.
text.NRSV: The priests and the Levites and the Israelites settled in Jerusalem and in the country. On the new moon of the seventh month the Israelites were in their settlements.
text.DRB: And the Priests and the Levites, and they that were of Israel, dwelt in Jerusalem, and in the whole country in the newmoon of the seventh month. And the children of Israel were in their habitations.
And all the multitude was gathered together into the court, which is on the east of the sacred gate:
text.KJV: And the whole multitude came together with one accord into the broad place of the holy porch toward the east:
text.NRSV: the whole multitude gathered with one accord in the open square before the east gate of the temple;

text.KJV: And they spake unto Esdras the priest and reader, that he would bring the law of Moses, that was given of the Lord God of Israel.
text.NRSV: They told Ezra the chief priest and reader to bring the law of Moses that had been given by the Lord God of Israel.
text.DRB: and they said to Esdras the high priest, and reader, that he should bring the law of Moyses, which was delivered of our Lord the God of Israel.
And Esdras the high priest brought the law to all the multitude of them from man unto woman, and to all the priests to hear the law in the newmoon of the seventh month.
text.KJV: So Esdras the chief priest brought the law unto the whole multitude from man to woman, and to all the priests, to hear law in the first day of the seventh month.
text.NRSV: So Ezra the chief priest brought the law, for all the multitude, men and women, and all the priests to hear the law, at the new moon of the seventh month.

text.DRB: And he read in the court, which is before the sacred gate of the temple, from break of day until evening before men and women. And they all gave their mind to the law.
text.KJV: And he read in the broad court before the holy porch from morning unto midday, before both men and women; and the multitude gave heed unto the law.
text.NRSV: He read aloud in the open square before the gate of the temple from early morning until midday, in the presence of both men and women; and all the multitude gave attention to the law.

text.DRB: And Esdras the priest, and reader of the law stood upon a tribunal of wood, which was made.
text.KJV: And Esdras the priest and reader of the law stood up upon a pulpit of wood, which was made for that purpose.
text.NRSV: Ezra the priest and reader of the law stood on the wooden platform that had been prepared;

text.DRB: And by him stood Mathathias, and Samus, and Ananias, Azarias, Urias, Ezechias, and Balsamus on the right hand,
text.KJV: And there stood up by him Mattathias, Sammus, Ananias, Azarias, Urias, Ezecias, Balasamus, upon the right hand:
text.NRSV: and beside him stood Mattathiah, Shema, Ananias, Azariah, Uriah, Hezekiah, and Baalsamus on his right,

text.DRB: and on the left Faldeus, Misael, Malachias, Ambusthas, Sabus, Nabadias, and Zacharias.
text.KJV: And upon his left hand stood Phaldaius, Misael, Melchias, Lothasubus, and Nabarias.
text.NRSV: and on his left Pedaiah, Mishael, Malchijah, Lothasubus, Nabariah, and Zechariah.

text.DRB: And Esdras took the book before all the multitude: for he was chief in glory in the sight of all.
text.KJV: Then took Esdras the book of the law before the multitude: for he sat honourably in the first place in the sight of them all.
text.NRSV: Then Ezra took up the book of the law in the sight of the multitude, for he had the place of honour in the presence of all.

text.KJV: And when he opened the law, they stood all straight up. So Esdras blessed the Lord God most High, the God of hosts, Almighty.
text.NRSV: Ezra blessed the Lord God Most High, the God of hosts, the Almighty.
text.DRB: And when he had ended the law, they stood all upright: and Esdras blessed our Lord the most high God, the God of Sabaoth omnipotent.

text.KJV: And all the people answered, Amen; and lifting up their hands they fell to the ground, and worshipped the Lord.
text.NRSV: And all the people answered, “Amen!” And they lifted up their hands and fell to the ground and worshiped the Lord.
text.DRB: And all the people answered: Amen. And lifting up their hands falling on the ground, they adored our Lord.
Iesus and Banæus, and Sarebias, and Iaddimus, and Accubus, and Sabbathæus, and Calithes, & Azarias, and Ioradus, and Ananias, and Philias Levites,
text.KJV: Also Jesus, Anus, Sarabias, Adinus, Jacubus, Sabateas, Auteas, Maianeas, and Calitas, Asrias, and Joazabdus, and Ananias, Biatas, the Levites, taught the law of the Lord, making them withal to understand it.
text.NRSV: Jeshua and Anniuth and Sherebiah, Jadinus, Akkub, Shabbethai, Hodiah, Maiannas and Kelita, Azariah and Jozabad, Hanan, Pelaiah, the Levites, taught the law of the Lord,] at the same time explaining what was read.

text.DRB: who taught the law of our Lord, and read the same in the multitude, & every one preferred them that understood the lesson.
text.KJV: Then spake Attharates unto Esdras the chief priest. and reader, and to the Levites that taught the multitude, even to all, saying,
text.NRSV: Then Attharates] said to Ezra the chief priest and reader, and to the Levites who were teaching the multitude, and to all,

text.DRB: And Atharathes said to Esdras the high priest and the reader, and to the Levites, that taught the multitude,
text.KJV: This day is holy unto the Lord; (for they all wept when they heard the law:)
text.NRSV: ‘This day is holy to the Lord’—now they were all weeping as they heard the law—

text.DRB: saying: This day is sanctified to our Lord. And they all wept, when they had heard the law.
text.KJV: Go then, and eat the fat, and drink the sweet, and send part to them that have nothing;
text.NRSV: ‘so go your way, eat the fat and drink the sweet, and send portions to those who have none;

text.DRB: And Esdras said, departing therefore eat ye all the fattest things, & drink all most sweet things, and send gifts to them that have not.
text.KJV: For this day is holy unto the Lord: and be not sorrowful; for the Lord will bring you to honour.
text.NRSV: for the day is holy to the Lord; and do not be sorrowful, for the Lord will exalt you.’

text.DRB: For this is the holy day of our Lord, & be not sad. For our Lord will glorify you.
text.KJV: So the Levites published all things to the people, saying, This day is holy to the Lord; be not sorrowful.
text.NRSV: The Levites commanded all the people, saying, ‘This day is holy; do not be sorrowful.’

text.DRB: And the Levites denounced openly to all, saying: This day is holy, be not sad.
text.KJV: Then went they their way, every one to eat and drink, and make merry, and to give part to them that had nothing, and to make great cheer;
text.NRSV: Then they all went their way, to eat and drink and enjoy themselves, and to give portions to those who had none, and to make great rejoicing;

text.DRB: And they went all to eat, and drink, and make merry, and to give gifts to them that had not, that they might make merry, for they were exceedingly exalted with the words that they were taught.
text.KJV: Because they understood the words wherein they were instructed, and for the which they had been assembled.
text.NRSV: because they were inspired by the words which they had been taught. And they came together.]

text.DRB: And they were all gathered in Jerusalem to celebrate the joy, according to the testament of our Lord the God of Israel.
